description: E2E test case designs for opencode-core session module - Conversation session management

test_cases.title: Session creation with fork and lineage tracking
test_cases.description: Test session fork preserves messages and maintains parent-child relationship
expected_outcomes: Child session has correct message subset
lineage_path computed correctly for nested forks

test_cases.title: Undo/redo message history
test_cases.description: Test undo and redo operations on session messages
steps: Call undo(5) - verify error (not enough to undo)
expected_outcomes: Undo removes correct number of messages
Redo stack clears after new message added

test_cases.title: Session sharing with expiry
test_cases.description: Test share link generation and expiry handling
expected_outcomes: Expired shares are reported as not shared

test_cases.title: Session export to JSON and Markdown
test_cases.description: Test session serialization formats with credential redaction
steps: Create session with messages including tool invocations
Add messages with potential sensitive content: URLs, API keys, tokens
Parse JSON output and verify structure
Verify sensitive content is redacted in both formats
expected_outcomes: JSON output is valid and deserializable
Credentials redacted in export (API keys, tokens, passwords)
Markdown has role headers and formatted content
edge_cases: Message with base64-encoded content (should not be decoded and redacted)
Message with JSON string containing API key

test_cases.title: Fork at message index boundary errors
steps: Call fork_at_message(-1) or underflow - expect error
Call fork_at_message(0) - fork with empty message set
expected_outcomes: Fork at 0 creates child with no messages

test_cases.title: Tool invocation recording
test_cases.description: Test tool invocations are recorded in session with hash and latency
steps: Verify args_hash is SHA-256 of args JSON
expected_outcomes: Tool invocation recorded with all fields

test_cases.title: Session compaction via Compactor
steps: Run compaction with target message count
expected_outcomes: Tool invocations before compaction still accessible

test_cases.title: Concurrent session fork and undo operations
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Verify session remains consistent under concurrent fork/undo operations
test_cases.risk_addressed: Data corruption or inconsistent state from concurrent modifications
steps: Verify both operations completed without panic
Verify session state is consistent (message count valid)
expected_outcomes: fork_at_message and undo don't race to corrupting state
post_checks: Session can still accept new messages

test_cases.title: Session compaction during active agent iteration
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Verify session compaction does not corrupt state when agent is running
test_cases.risk_addressed: Data loss or corruption when compaction runs concurrently with agent
steps: Start agent.run() which will iterate many times
While agent running, trigger compaction concurrently
Verify session state is consistent after completion
expected_outcomes: Session has valid message history after compaction
No messages silently deleted during iteration

test_cases.title: Undo does not cross fork boundary
test_cases.description: Undo must not remove messages from parent session
test_cases.risk_addressed: Undo removing messages from parent session after fork
test_cases.preconditions: Session A with 5 messages, Session B forked from A at index 3
steps: In child session B (3 messages), call undo(10)
Verify either error OR only child's 3 messages removed
Verify parent session A still has all 5 messages
expected_outcomes: Undo limited to session's own messages
Child session undo count respects available messages
post_checks: Parent session still has exactly 5 messages

test_cases.title: Storage file corruption recovery
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Verify system handles corrupted session storage gracefully
steps: Write invalid bytes to a session storage file (simulate corruption)
Attempt to load the corrupted session
expected_outcomes: Error message is descriptive (parse error, not generic)
post_checks: Storage file for corrupted session can be removed manually

test_cases.title: Message ordering guaranteed after fork and undo
test_cases.description: Verify message ordering is preserved after complex fork/undo sequences
test_cases.risk_addressed: Message reordering or duplication after fork/undo operations
steps: Create session with messages: [A, B, C, D, E] (in order)
Fork at index 3 -> child has [A, B, C]
Undo(1) on child -> [A, B, C, F]
Verify message order is [A, B, C, F]
Verify parent still has [A, B, C, D, E]
expected_outcomes: Message sequence is exactly [A, B, C, F]

test_cases.title: Concurrent session forks from same parent
test_cases.description: Multiple forks created simultaneously from same parent must not interfere
test_cases.risk_addressed: Fork ID collision or parent state corruption
steps: Spawn 10 threads simultaneously, each calling fork_at_message(3)
Verify all child IDs are unique
Verify each child has exactly 3 messages
Verify parent still has 5 messages and unchanged
expected_outcomes: All child IDs are distinct UUIDs
No ID collision or race condition
post_checks: Children can each be forked again

test_cases.title: Session export redaction completeness
test_cases.description: Verify ALL sensitive content is redacted in exports, not just obvious API keys
test_cases.risk_addressed: Sensitive data leakage in exported sessions
steps: Create session with messages containing each sensitive pattern
Verify no sensitive value appears in plaintext
expected_outcomes: Redaction marker consistent across all types
post_checks: Exported session is still valid JSON
Exported session can be imported back

critical_invariants: Concurrent operations do not corrupt state
Compaction does not lose data during agent iteration
